header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Learning & Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing learning programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact—always helping people reach proficiency faster.

bullets.text: Manage {prog|enablement programs} end-to-end for 20+ initiatives—timelines, milestones, and delivery across Marketing, Tech, and Sales
{fac|Facilitate} training webinars for 600+ participants, designing {id|live sessions, async materials, and SOPs}, achieving a 78% enrollment rate
Build and maintain {km|documentation and knowledge systems} for 70+ staff, auditing and updating materials to reflect process changes
Drive adoption by interviewing users to find performance gaps, then designing targeted {id|just-in-time} interventions
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed L&D strategy
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to {data|measure} satisfaction drivers and guide C-suite decisions
Provided consultations and internal {prog|training} on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Delivered 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects spanning survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Designed and piloted an Inclusive Leadership {prog|training program} for senior leaders, scaled to 23 global locations
Optimized onboarding for new hires across 3 global locations by analyzing the process and clarifying responsibilities
Partnered with a Denmark DEI {id|SME}, building workshop content, practice exercises, and follow-up reinforcement
Strengthened {cross|engagement} via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across HR functions, from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Drafted job descriptions and supporting materials for a challenging international role
Engaged global talent via personalized {cross|communication} and service, lifting applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized events and {fac|moderated panels} with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students
As a team of consultants, partnered with an HR client to audit 300+ leave cases, quantifying $362K to refute assumptions
Empowered the HR team to present executive-ready {data|data} insights, driving policy redesigns and first aid training

jd.meta: Mercury — Customer Support Learning & Enablement Specialist

changeLog: 2026-05-31: Tailored for Mercury — Customer Support Learning & Enablement Specialist. Broad umbrella 'Learning & Development.' Location → Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation — role lists Portland + remote US). Strong fit. Led InGenius with end-to-end enablement programs (timelines/milestones), 600-participant facilitation + multi-format content (live/async/SOPs), knowledge-mgmt documentation w/ audit-and-update, and gap-driven just-in-time interventions. Vestas surfaces instructional design (SME partnership, practice exercises, reinforcement = real ADKAR story). Skills include Notion + SharePoint (JD names knowledge-mgmt; Guru/Notion mentioned). 78% program enrollment rate (L&D). ODN collective. Counts fixed (InGenius/NextGen/Vestas/Kronos=4, ODN=2).
